task_name: N/A - Educational presentation on remote FLS training
presentation_title: Remote Instruction of Fundamentals of Laparoscopic Skills through Video Streaming
summary: This is an educational presentation about delivering FLS training remotely using videoconferencing software (OBS Studio, Zoom) with FLS trainer boxes. The content includes disclosures, case studies from University of Toronto (Botswana 2010, Toronto 2020), discussion of virtual cam setup, remote training data (summative time and task improvement metrics), and conclusions about effectiveness of remote FLS instruction. No actual FLS task performance is demonstrated or scored.

key_content_sections.content: No current or past relationships with commercial entities
University of Toronto remote FLS training programs (Botswana 2010 in-person vs Toronto 2020 remote delivery)
Virtual cam using OBS Studio and Zoom with audio input/output configuration

Summative time data pre/post showing improvement trajectory; task-specific time comparisons (Peg Transfer 10s→70s, Circle Cut 61s→157s, Endoloop 47s→165s, Extracorporeal 147s→125s, Intracorporeal 88s→177s)

FLS skills training can be effectively delivered remotely using videoconferencing software with FLS box, laptop, and web-camera. Recommendation to look to other industries for translatable technologies to advance educational content.

reason_no_score: This is a conference presentation/lecture about remote FLS training methodology, not a demonstration of an actual FLS task performance. The video contains instructional content, research data, and conclusions about remote training delivery, but no hands-on task execution to score.

demonstrated_techniques: Remote FLS trainer setup using OBS Studio virtual camera
Zoom videoconferencing integration for real-time instruction
Multi-camera view arrangement (task view, streamer view, commentary box)
verbal_cues: Educational slides on remote instruction feasibility
Data-driven presentation of pre/post performance metrics
Discussion of summative time improvements across all five FLS tasks
common_errors_addressed: This presentation demonstrates that remote FLS instruction does NOT result in significantly worse performance compared to in-person training
teaching_content.context: Educational research presentation aimed at surgical educators and residents/fellows attending SAGES 2021 conference

confidence_rationale: Video classification is unambiguous—this is clearly a conference presentation slide deck with no task performance video. Content is educational/instructional in nature.
